description: Synthetic benchmark dataset for CaSVeM v3. Tests personal AI memory across 5 categories. Created to demonstrate retrieval accuracy, cache warmup, and token savings at scale.

memories.text: User's name is Arjun Sharma. He goes by Arjun.
Arjun is a backend software engineer at a Series B fintech startup in Bangalore.
Arjun has 5 years of experience. Primarily works in Python and Go.
Arjun lives in Indiranagar, Bangalore with his wife Priya.
Arjun prefers dark mode in all IDEs and terminals. Uses VS Code with vim keybindings.
Arjun drinks black coffee every morning, no sugar. Allergic to milk.
Arjun's favorite programming language is Python for ML work, Go for systems.
Arjun is vegetarian. Favorite food is dosa and sambar.
Arjun runs 5km every Tuesday and Thursday morning before work.
Arjun meditates for 10 minutes every evening using Headspace app.
Arjun's current project: building a payment reconciliation microservice using Kafka and PostgreSQL.
Arjun is learning Rust in his spare time. Currently on Chapter 8 of the Rust Book.
Arjun's team uses GitHub for version control, Jira for project tracking, Slack for communication.
Arjun's annual salary review is in September. He wants to negotiate a 20% raise.
Arjun is applying to Y Combinator with his side project CaSVeM — an AI memory system.
Arjun's three-year goal: become a founding engineer at an AI startup or start his own.
Arjun has been reading Designing Data-Intensive Applications by Martin Kleppmann.
Last week Arjun attended PyCon India 2026 in Hyderabad. Talked with several ML engineers.
Arjun's GitHub handle is arjunsharma-dev. He has 847 public contributions this year.
Arjun's laptop: MacBook Pro M3 at work. Personal machine: ThinkPad X1 Carbon running Ubuntu 24.04.
